title: Gektl
content:

{{Quote|Unh! These Gektls are ''tough''–!|Luke Skywalker, while fighting Major Stafuv Rahz|<ref name="Bring Me the Children">"[[Bring Me the Children]]"</ref>}}
'''Gektls'''—occasionally spelled '''Gecktls'''—were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of bipedal [[Reptile/Legends|reptilians]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoszh Iszhir]]. They were regarded as tough survivors, and members of the species were capable of sloughing off their skin and even [[Limb/Legends|tails]] as a means of escaping danger. They were a long-established part of [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] society, known for their artistic and literary skill. Their [[Gektl painted egg|painted eggs]] in particular were regarded galaxywide as beautiful and highly collectible artworks. The Gektl [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was protected with a [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]] to defend its bounty of strategic metals from would-be invaders.

[[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Stafuv Rahz]] of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] was a member of this species. Rahz betrayed his planet to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], providing them with the codes necessary to breach Hoszh Iszhir's shield in exchange for his commission; he was later placed in charge of an operation designed to lure and trap [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] by executing a [[Myoris Lin|schoolteacher]] and her students.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Rahz-tail.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Major Rahz loses his tail to escape capture.]]
{{Quote|Yi–! His tail broke off!|A Rebel [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] attempting to restrain Stafuv Rahz|<ref name="Bring Me the Children" />}}
The Gektls were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], bipedal [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] that stood with a hunched, knee-bent stance. Their dorsal scales were [[Color/Legends|green]], while the scales on their underbellies were yellow. Their four-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]] were [[Claw/Legends|clawed]] and their feet had three clawed toes and a clawed heel. They had long, sinuous tails wrapped in concentric scales that peaked on top, giving them a jagged appearance. Gektl faces were long and slightly flattened, and the tops of their heads possessed three small crests of scales. They had yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and a long, narrow, [[pink]] tongue.<ref name="CSW:TEA7" />

The Gektl [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Hoszh Iszhir]], was a dangerous place; one hemisphere was a barren desert, the other was covered with a poisonous [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and both sides were filled with deadly predators. As a result, Gektls were by necessity born survivors.<ref name="AitE2" /> The reptilians were hardy and physically resilient creatures. A Gektl's natural defenses included the ability to shed skin that had been grabbed, revealing the lighter green skin beneath; they were also able to break off their tails.<ref name="CSW:TEA7" /> The sloughed skin could even be used to create decoys.<ref name="GG3">[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]]</ref>

==Society and culture==
Despite their aptitude for survival, Gektls were renowned for millennia for their literature and other artistic endeavors. Particularly valuable were [[Gektl painted egg]]s, which were some of the most highly prized collectibles in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Each [[Egg/Legends|egg]], when lit properly, conveyed scenes of significant Gektl historical events.<ref name="AitE2" /> When Gektls showed their [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] in what appeared to be a smile, the expression was considered to be a challenge.<ref name="FA">''[[The Old Republic: Fatal Alliance]]''</ref>

==History==
Gektls were natives of Hoszh Iszhir,<ref name="AitE2" /> a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Gekto system]].<ref name="Unstated Canon">{{Blog|danwallace|153|Unstated Canon in Star Wars: The Essential Atlas}}</ref> Located in the [[Uoti sector/Legends|Uoti sector]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]],<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> the planet became a part of wider galactic civilization in the early years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]].<ref name="AitE2" /> The planet was rich in precious metals such as [[Guerrerite/Legends|guerrerite]], and the Gektls established a [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]] system to defend their world and its riches<ref name="AitE2" /> many generations before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|61|Hoszh Iszhir}}</ref>

After [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] rise to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]], he desired the resources of Hoszh Iszhir. He was given the opportunity to fulfill his desire when [[Stafuv Rahz]], maintainer of the planet's shield system, traveled to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and sought an audience with the Emperor. Rahz offered Palpatine the codes to the shields in exchange for a position of power; Palpatine granted him an [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] commission and an assignment to keep the planets in the Gekto [[Star system/Legends|system]] in line. The Emperor then sent a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] to bombard Hoszh Iszhir into submission, [[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] the Gektls and forcing them to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] the world's resources.<ref name="AitE2" />

==Gektl in the galaxy==
{{Quote|An unsubstantiated claim from a Gecktl ''[sic]'' criminal who escaped the droid indicates that IG-88 has adopted a new tactic.|[[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]]|<ref name="GG3" />}}
During the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] following the signing of the [[Treaty of Coruscant]], the female Gektl [[Dao Stryver]], a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]], attended an auction held by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Tassaa Bareesh]], where the Hutt was holding a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] and another mysterious object up for sale. Stryver [[Skirmish in Tassaa Bareesh's palace|fought]] with the other attendees for the prize, eventually taking the navicomp and locating the world of origin from which the other mysterious object—by then revealed to be a nest of [[Fastbreeder|hex droids]], a dangerous and deadly type of droid—had come. There, the Gektl convinced the Republic and [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Imperial]] forces to ally for a [[Independent Operation Sebaddon|mission to destroy]] the hexes before they could take control of the galaxy. The mission proved to be a success, and the hex droids were eliminated.<ref name="FA" />

[[File:Rahz-skin.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Major Rahz sloughs his skin to escape Rebel smugglers, a Gektl ability later emulated by IG-88.]]
During the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], many Gektls were enslaved and put to work pulling ore from their homeworld's mines. One exception was Rahz, who, following the betrayal of his people, became a [[Major/Legends|major]] in the Imperial Military.<ref name="AitE2" /> He supervised a raid of the mist-enshrouded world of [[Kar'a'katok]] and the capture of a native [[Shimholt]], [[Suprema|Ampotem Za]], who eventually became the slaver known as the "Suprema."<ref name="AitE1">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/96/indexp2.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 1: Hierarchy of Power}}</ref>

Rahz continued serving in the Empire, and he became embroiled in an Imperial operation on [[Harix]]. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] sent him to kidnap the [[Teacher/Legends|teacher]] [[Myoris Lin]] and her students, but her son [[Berd Lin|Berd]] escaped with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Vader ordered Rahz to broadcast word that the captives would be executed as bait for a trap intended to capture members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]]. Skywalker brought Berd to a Rebel-allied [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], where the child recruited Rebel [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] to carry out a rescue plan that used [[Deep Space Target Practice Unit|target drones]] as decoys to lure the Imperials out of position. Berd also warned the Rebels of the Imperials' plans to trap them after he was made aware of it through a [[Dream/Legends|dream]], and Skywalker and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] destroyed Rahz's ambush force hidden behind the [[Talhu Slotted Peaks]]. Rahz, faced with the loss of this force and the jamming of comm channels, attempted to carry out the executions with his [[sonic beam projector]] weapon, but he was jumped by Skywalker and restrained by the Rebel smugglers, who had defeated Rahz's [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Rahz shed his clothes, skin, and tail to escape them, and rushed to the projector. Berd rephased the power of the weapon, though, and when Rahz attempted to fire it, he was killed.<ref name="CSW:TEA7" />

Other Gektls became criminals. [[Unidentified Gektl criminal|One such criminal]] claimed to have been the target of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[IG-88B/Legends|IG-88]]; he managed to escape by shedding his skin, allegedly inspiring IG-88 to fabricate decoy shells to fake his destruction.<ref name="GG3" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|As he did with the Shimholt, Rich did an amazing job fleshing out the culture of the Gektls in so few words. It goes without saying that it's much harder to develop a species from scratch than it is to borrow a pre-existing one.|Abel G. Peña|<ref name="TFN1">{{JCFCite|url=posts/27825175|board=Literature|thread=Aliens in the Empire (Part 2 now up)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2007-12-05|time=5:00 PM|quote=As he did with the Shimholt, Rich did an amazing job fleshing out the culture of the Gektls in so few words. It goes without saying that it's much harder to develop a species from scratch than it is to borrow a pre-existing one.|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801202856/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/aliens-of-the-empire-part-2-now-up.27707732/page-5#post-27825175|archivedate=20200801202856}}</ref>}}
The Gektls were created by [[Russ Manning]] in the form of Imperial Major Rahz in ''[[Bring Me the Children]]'', an arc of the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' comic strip]] published in [[1980]] and reprinted in [[Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures 7|''Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures'' 7]] in [[1995]]. Other than reprints of Manning's [[Star Wars comic books|comic]], Gektls were referred to only once in two and a half decades, as a brief aside in the [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|second edition]] of ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' in [[1996]]; in that book, the species was spelled "'''Gecktl'''."

In [[2007]], ''[[Aliens in the Empire]]'', an article written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Rich Handley]], was released in two parts in the online supplements to [[Star Wars Insider 96|''Star Wars Insider'' 96]] and [[Star Wars Insider 97|97]]. The first part briefly revisits Stafuv Rahz in the context of his capture of Apotem Za, the Suprema; part two includes a full write-up of Rahz, which includes information on the Gektl homeworld and culture. These details, like much of Rahz's biography, were the creations of Handley.<ref name="TFN1" /> The species is mentioned in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]], and a Gektl character, Dao Stryver, appeared in the novel ''[[The Old Republic: Fatal Alliance]]'' in [[2010]].
